zh.Grave, Brabant, Netherlands: 荷蘭努-布拉班特格拉福
zh.lifeSummary: 當Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke於1240年1月1日出生在荷蘭-拉藍德蒙斯特時，他的父親Hendrik I VAN DER LECKE25歲，母親Jutta VAN WERTH25歲。他於1271年10月30日與Jutta van Borssele在荷蘭-拉藍德蒙斯特結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1305年6月12日之後在荷蘭霍蘭蒙斯特。

zh-hans.Grave, Brabant, Netherlands: 荷兰努-布拉班特格拉福
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke于1240年1月1日出生在荷兰-拉藍德蒙斯特时，他的父亲Hendrik I VAN DER LECKE25岁，母亲Jutta VAN WERTH25岁。他于1271年10月30日与Jutta van Borssele在荷兰-拉藍德蒙斯特结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1305年6月12日之后在荷兰霍蘭蒙斯特。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Lecke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Heck   1.
American shortened form of Flemish and Dutch   Van Hecke  .

variants.name: Heck
meanings: South German: topographic name from Middle High German hecke, hegge ‘hedge’. This surname is common in southern Germany and the Rhineland, and is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).
Dutch: topographic name from Middle Dutch heck ‘fence, gate’. Compare Van Hecke .
English: topographic name for someone who lived by a gate or ‘hatch’ (especially one leading into a forest), northern Middle English heck (Old English hæcce). Compare Hatch .

nameMeanings.name: Hendrick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: In modern use a transferred use of the surname, the origins of which are complex. The source in many cases is the Old Welsh personal name Cynwrig. This is of uncertain derivation: it may be composed of elements meaning ‘high, exalted’ + ‘hill’ or ‘summit’. The Scottish surname Ken(d)rick is a shortened form of MacKen(d)rick (Gaelic Mac Eanraig ‘son of Henry’); Scottish bearers are descended from a certain Henry MacNaughton, and the (Mac)Ken(d)ricks are a sept of Clan MacNaughton. As an English surname, Ken(d)rick is derived, at least in part, from the Middle English given name Cenric, in which two Old English personal names have fallen together: Cēnerīc (from cēne ‘keen’ + rīc ‘power’) and Cynerīc (from cyne ‘royal’ + rīc ‘power’). Cenric survived as a given name into the 17th century.

variants.name: Kenrick
meanings: Contracted form of   Kendrick .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Wondrous’ or ‘enlightenment’.
2  ♀ ‘Fine jade’.

en.lifeSummary: When Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke was born on 1 January 1240, in Monster, South Holland, Netherlands, his father, Hendrik I VAN DER LECKE, was 25 and his mother, Jutta VAN WERTH, was 25. He married Jutta van Borssele on 30 October 1271, in Monster, South Holland, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He died after 12 June 1305, in Monster, Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke est né le 1 janvier 1240, à Monster, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Hendrik I VAN DER LECKE, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Jutta VAN WERTH, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Jutta van Borssele le 30 octobre 1271, à Monster, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé après 12 juin 1305, à Monster, Holland, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke am 1. Januar 1240 in Monster, Südholland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hendrik I VAN DER LECKE 25 und seine Mutter Jutta VAN WERTH 25. Er hat am 30. Oktober 1271 in Monster, Südholland, Niederlande Jutta van Borssele geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb nach 12. Juni 1305 in Monster, Holland, Niederlande.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke è nato il il 1 gennaio 1240 nel Monster, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Hendrik I VAN DER LECKE, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Jutta VAN WERTH, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Jutta van Borssele il 30 ottobre 1271, nel Monster, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì dopo il 12 giugno 1305, nel Monster, Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.Martin, Žilinský kraj, Slovakia: スロバキアマルティン
ja.lifeSummary: Heer Hendrick II van der Leckeが1240年1月1日にオランダズイド-hオランドモンスターで生まれた時，父Hendrik I VAN DER LECKEは25歳，母Jutta VAN WERTHは25歳であった。1271年10月30日にオランダズイド-hオランドモンスターでJutta van Borsseleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1305年6月12日以後にオランダホーランドモンスターで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke(이)가 1240년 1월 1일 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 몬스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hendrik I VAN DER LECKE(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Jutta VAN WERTH(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1271년 10월 30일 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 몬스터에서 Jutta van Borssele(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1305년 6월 12일 이후 네덜란드 홀랜드 몬스터에서 사망했다.

pt.Grave, Brabant, Netherlands: Grave, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1240, em Monster, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Hendrik I VAN DER LECKE, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Jutta VAN WERTH, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Jutta van Borssele em 30 de outubro de 1271, em Monster, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu após 12 de junho de 1305, em Monster, Holland, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke родился 1 января 1240 г. в местности монстир, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Hendrik I VAN DER LECKE было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Jutta VAN WERTH, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Jutta van Borssele 30 октября 1271 г. в местности монстир, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер после 12 июнь 1305 г. в местности монстир, холланд, Нидерланды.
